mission.title: langflow: 🐛 Bug Report: Frontend Black Screen with Missing Chinese Locale File Error
mission.description: 🐛 Bug Report: Frontend Black Screen with Missing Chinese Locale File Error. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify langflow troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your langflow installation:
```bash
langflow --version
```
Look for errors or warnings that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review langflow configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant langflow configuration:
### Bug Description

## **Description**
When accessing Langflow with a browser that has Chinese (zh) as the preferred language, the frontend displays a black screen and fails to load.

steps.title: Apply the fix for 🐛 Bug Report: Frontend Black Screen with Missing Chinese…
steps.description:
Hi everybody!

We're aware of this issue — it is still reproducible on `v1.9.2` — and it has already been fixed in [`21492cc`](https://github.com/langflow-ai/langflow/commit/21492cc). The fix centralizes all locale detection through `normalizeLanguage`, which means any unsupported browser language
```yaml
index-CuWAH0LD.js:980 Uncaught (in promise) Error: Unknown variable dynamic import: ./locales/zh.json
    at index-CuWAH0LD.js:980:3787
    at new Promise (<anonymous>)
    at rwr (index-CuWAH0LD.js:980:3693)
    at Gwr (index-CuWAH0LD.js:980:82055)
    at index-CuWAH0LD.js:7102:13430
```

steps.title: Upgrade langflow to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade langflow:
```bash
# Check current version
langflow --version
# Follow the project's upgrade guide at https://github.com/langflow-ai/langflow
```

steps.title: Confirm 🐛 Bug Report: Frontend Black Screen with Missing… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
Test langflow to confirm the issue is resolved.
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
Hi everybody!

We're aware of this issue — it is still reproducible on `v1.9.2` — and it has already been fixed in [`21492cc`](https://github.com/langflow-ai/langflow/commit/21492cc). The fix centralizes all locale detection through `normalizeLanguage`, which means any unsupported browser language (not just Chinese) now falls back to English gracefully instead of crashing.
codeSnippets:
